title: Velvet Broccoli Delight
description: A buttery, velvety Peruvian-inspired dish featuring Chinese broccoli, seasoned with aromatic spices and simmered until tender.

ingredients.name: Chinese Broccoli
ingredients.notes: Use the florets, as they are tender and flavorful.

ingredients.name: Garlic Cloves
ingredients.notes: Mince finely for better flavor integration.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chopped into fine pieces for a rich texture.

ingredients.name: Shallots
ingredients.notes: Finely chopped to infuse more flavor.

ingredients.name: Coconut Milk
ingredients.notes: To achieve the desired velvety texture and smoothness.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: For umami and depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Ground Cumin
ingredients.notes: To add a warm, earthy note to the dish.

instructions: Mince the garlic and shallots finely.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add minced shallots and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic, cumin, and sliced onion. Stir-fry for an additional 5 minutes or until onions are slightly caramelized.
Pour in coconut milk and soy sauce (and fish sauce if using). Stir well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cover with a lid and let it cook for about 10-12 minutes. The liquid should reduce significantly.
Add Chinese broccoli florets to the pan. Toss gently so that they don't break apart too much.
Simmer for another 5-7 minutes or until the broccoli is very tender, occasionally stirring to prevent sticking.
Season with salt and pepper according to taste.
Serve hot as a side dish or over steamed rice for an authentic Peruvian-Chinese fusion experience.
